articles.headline: Oklahoma principal shot in leg is praised for tackling school shooter: 'He is a hero'
articles.image_alt: Pauls Valley High School Principal Kirk Moore
articles.excerpt: Principal Kirk Moore tackled a 20-year-old gunman at Pauls Valley High School on April 7, disarming him with the help of an assistant principal. Moore was shot in the leg during the struggle but held the suspect until police arrived.

articles.headline: Bill advances to protect teachers, but critics warn of impact on students of color and rural parishes
articles.excerpt: Louisiana House Bill 283 — the Teacher Shield Act — cleared the House on April 8 and is now under Senate review. The bill would require immediate removal and alternative placement for students who assault teachers, plus expanded leave for educator recovery.

articles.headline: Spanberger signs bipartisan school-safety, student support bills into Virginia law
articles.image_alt: Virginia Governor signing school safety legislation
articles.excerpt: Governor Abigail Spanberger signed a package of bipartisan bills on April 2, including HB592 to let districts equip teachers with wearable panic alarms, HB38 to train teachers on student mental health, and HB1071 strengthening red-flag training.

articles.headline: Student dead after shooting teacher at Hill Country Preparatory High School in Comal ISD
articles.image_alt: Law enforcement at the scene of a school shooting
articles.excerpt: A 15-year-old student shot a teacher at Hill Country College Preparatory High School in Bulverde, Texas on March 30, then turned the gun on himself, according to the Comal County Sheriff's Office. The teacher survived with injuries.

articles.headline: Teacher hospitalized after student altercation at Newburgh Free Academy
articles.excerpt: A teacher at Newburgh Free Academy in New York was hospitalized at Montefiore St. Luke's Cornwall Hospital after being assaulted by a student during what the district called a 'mental health episode.' Police and emergency services responded to the 11:30 a.m. classroom incident.

articles.headline: From chaos to fear: How classroom violence is affecting students and teachers
articles.excerpt: KSAT's investigative team documents how classroom violence is pushing experienced Texas educators out of the profession. Teachers describe being grabbed, kicked, punched, spat at, and threatened. Texas has lost nearly 5,000 teachers over the past two school years.

articles.headline: Dozens of first responders flood Valley Forge High School after cafeteria shooting
articles.image_alt: Emergency responders at Valley Forge High School
articles.excerpt: A student died after a shooting in the cafeteria of Valley Forge High School in Parma Heights, Ohio on April 20. The incident prompted an immediate lockdown and response from dozens of first responders from across the region.

articles.headline: South Carolina lawmakers introduce 'Educator Safety and Classroom Authority Act of 2026'
articles.excerpt: House Bill 5483, the Educator Safety and Classroom Authority Act of 2026, was introduced April 1 and referred to the House Committee on Education and Public Works. The bill establishes educator authority in classroom discipline and protections from disruptive and violent student behavior.

articles.headline: Teacher charged after hoax triggers lockdown at Splendora High School, sheriff says
articles.excerpt: A Splendora, Texas high school teacher was charged with filing a false report and tampering with evidence after investigators determined that an alleged student assault that triggered a campus lockdown on April 9 was a hoax with self-inflicted injuries.

articles.headline: Capitol Access Minute: Legislation advances, along with Teacher Shield Act
articles.excerpt: Louisiana Public Radio's Capitol Access Minute covers the April 8 advancement of the Teacher Shield Act (HB 283) through the House. Rep. Candace Newell, a former teacher, authored the bill; Sen. Gary Carter will carry it in the Senate.
